---
title: MCP integration
description: Connect your AI client to Lomi with the Model Context Protocol.
---

import { Callout } from 'fumadocs-ui/components/callout';

# MCP integration

This page is for **developers** wiring an MCP-compatible client (Cursor, Claude Desktop, custom agents, etc.) to **Lomi’s merchant API** via our MCP server.

## What you need from your team

- **MCP base URL** — e.g. `https://your-mcp-host.example` (your org hosts it or gives you a shared URL).
- **Transport secret** (optional) — if they set `LOMI_MCP_BEARER_TOKEN`, you must send it as a Bearer token (see below).
- **Merchant API key** — a `lomi_*` secret used for tool calls (from the dashboard device flow or **Developers → API keys**).

## Endpoints

| Purpose | URL |
| --- | --- |
| MCP (Streamable HTTP) | `https://<host>/mcp` |
| Liveness | `https://<host>/health` |
| Readiness (config + manifest) | `https://<host>/ready` |

The MCP path may differ if your host sets a custom `LOMI_MCP_HTTP_PATH`.

## How to authenticate

**If a transport secret is configured on the server:**

- Send `Authorization: Bearer <transport-secret>` (your host may allow **comma-separated** rotation values server-side; you receive **one** secret to use).
- Send your merchant key as **`x-lomi-api-key`** or **`x-api-key`** — do not rely on `Authorization` for the merchant key in that case.

**If no transport secret is configured** (unusual in production):

- You may use `Authorization: Bearer <lomi_* merchant key>` for simple local setups, or still prefer `x-lomi-api-key`.

<Callout type="info">
  One deployment can serve **many merchants**: each user attaches their own key via **`x-lomi-api-key`** (not a single shared `LOMI_API_KEY` on the server).
</Callout>

## Get a `lomi_mcp_*` key (browser flow)

1. `POST https://<supabase-project>.supabase.co/functions/v1/mcp-auth/device-auth` — no body. Keep `device_code`, `user_code`, `verification_uri`.
2. Open **`verification_uri`** (dashboard path `/connect/mcp-verify`), sign in, enter **`user_code`**, authorize.
3. `POST https://<supabase-project>.supabase.co/functions/v1/mcp-auth/token` with JSON `{ "device_code": "<device_code>" }` — poll until you get `{ "api_key": "lomi_mcp_…" }` (pending responses use `authorization_pending`).
4. Configure your MCP client: **`x-lomi-api-key: <api_key>`** plus **`Authorization: Bearer <transport-secret>`** if your MCP host requires it.

Alternatively, use any valid **secret** key from **Developers → API keys** as `x-lomi-api-key`.

## npm package (`@lomi./mcp`)

```bash
npx -y @lomi./mcp
```

Stdio config templates live in the repo under `apps/mcp/examples/` (`cursor-stdio.mcp.json`, `cursor-http.mcp.json`, `claude-desktop-http.mcp.json`). Set **`LOMI_API_KEY`** (or **`X_API_KEY`**) and **`LOMI_API_BASE_URL`** (`https://api.lomi.africa` or `https://sandbox.api.lomi.africa`).

## Tool search, resources, and prompts

- **`lomi_search_tools`** — keyword search over the generated catalog when your client defers tool loading.
- **Resources** — `lomi://docs/getting-started`, `lomi://docs/errors`, `lomi://tools/index`.
- **Prompts** — `onboard_merchant`, `debug_failed_payment`, `setup_webhook`.

Tool metadata includes `readOnlyHint`, `destructiveHint`, and Composer-compatible `_meta` (`anthropic/searchHint`, `anthropic/alwaysLoad`).

## Dashboard connect panel

In **Developers → API keys**, use **Connect MCP** for copy-paste snippets. Set deployment env **`VITE_LOMI_MCP_URL`** to pre-fill your hosted MCP URL; merchants can edit it in the UI.

## Sandbox

Tool calls use whatever API base URL the **MCP server** is configured with (`LOMI_API_BASE_URL`). For sandbox data, that base URL must be **`https://sandbox.api.lomi.africa`**.
